top_signals.title: Bitcoin tops $60K amid Fed inflation talks: Is bull trap or $65K next?
top_signals.summary: Bitcoin rallied above $60,000 despite Federal Reserve rate hike fears and steady outflows from the BTC spot ETFs. Is the rally a bull trap?
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Kevin Warsh vows ‘no changes’ to Fed independence
top_signals.summary: New chair tells central bankers price stability will be focus regardless of potential pressure from White House
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Green Book changes to drive investment in all parts of UK
top_signals.summary: Communities that have been under-invested in and overlooked for decades are now getting a fair hearing as the Chancellor pushes forward with her overhaul of how investment decisions are made by government.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Lenders to Clearlake and TA-Backed Tungsten Sign Debt Pact
top_signals.summary: Lenders to Tungsten Automation have signed a pact agreeing to work together on a potential debt restructuring following a steep slide in the software company’s debt prices, according to people familiar with the matter.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: The Retail Payments Infrastructure Board launches consultation on the next generation UK payments infrastructure
top_signals.summary: Today marks a major milestone in the modernisation of the UK's payments landscape, with the Retail Payments Infrastructure Board (RPIB) launching a consultation on the future design of the UK's next-generation retail payments infrastructure.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Remaining anchored: Monetary Policy in an unpredictable world - speech by Andrew Bailey
top_signals.summary: Given at the Reykjavík Economic Conference 2026, Iceland
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Can AI make cutlery? -  speech by Andrew Bailey
top_signals.summary: Given at the 389th Cutlers’ Feast, Cutlers’ Hall, Sheffield
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.summary: Given at the 5th Conference on Financial Law and Regulation, University of Leeds School of Law, 24 June 2026
